beats.activity: Wake up, shower, make coffee at home
beats.reasoning: Yesterday's energy was moderate-low (45/100) after the sunset kayak; a quiet morning at home sets a grounded tone for a grey, rainy day. Wren doesn't rush on days like this.

beats.activity: Drive to Interbay lab through drizzle, listening to weather/news radio
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound closure 9am–noon won't affect Wren's westbound commute from Ballard to Interbay. Leaving early to avoid the rain intensity and get focused work done before the worst of the downpour.

beats.activity: Deep work on climate datasets and model runs; rain drumming on the lab windows
beats.reasoning: Steady rain from 7am–2pm is perfect for heads-down lab work. Wren thrives on focused analysis and won't be tempted to kayak or run errands in this weather. The SR-520 closure is irrelevant to her Ballard–Interbay routine.

beats.activity: Lunch at lab or nearby—probably a packed sandwich and coffee
beats.reasoning: Still raining; Wren is frugal and won't venture out for a fancy lunch. Staying put at the lab keeps momentum on work and saves time.

beats.activity: Afternoon meetings and admin; rain tapering toward 2pm
beats.reasoning: Post-lunch work session captures the tail end of the rain window. Team meetings and paperwork are good for low-energy, grey-day productivity.

beats.activity: Walk to nearby coffee stand or QFC for an afternoon coffee and snack
beats.reasoning: Rain cleared by 2pm; Wren's energy is still moderate. A short walk for coffee and a stretch break is low-key enough to fit the grey-day mood without overcommitting.

beats.activity: Return to lab, wrap up and reply to emails
beats.reasoning: Finishing the work day with loose ends. Wren prefers to leave work feeling caught-up, even if it's low-intensity.

beats.activity: Drive home through light post-rain traffic on 15th Ave W
beats.reasoning: Leaving Interbay heading west to Ballard is straightforward and unaffected by the SR-520 closure. Arriving home before 5pm gives Wren the evening.

beats.activity: Check weather forecast; if conditions clear, paddle Lake Union for sunset—otherwise stay home and game
beats.reasoning: Post-rain Seattle often clears by evening; Wren checks the sky. Yesterday's kayak recharged her, but today's low energy (45/100 carryover) and grey mood mean she's equally content to decompress indoors with video games if the weather doesn't cooperate.

beats.activity: Dinner: make a simple meal at home or order takeout from a trusted local spot
beats.reasoning: Wren is frugal and direct; she cooks or picks up something reliable rather than splurging. Eating at home keeps the day low-key and grounded.

diary: Woke to that familiar patter on the roof—rain all morning, steady and grey. Coffee tasted better than usual, maybe because I knew the commute would be a slog. Sure enough, SR-520 was a mess; I caught it on the radio and took 15th instead. The lab was cozy with the drumming overhead. I lost myself in the datasets for hours, which felt good. The models are showing some interesting variance in the summer projections. Grabbed a sandwich around noon and powered through meetings until the rain finally quit around two. Needed air, so I walked over to QFC for coffee and just... stood there for a minute, watching the clouds break apart. By the time I got home, the sky had that washed-clean quality. Lake Union was calling, so I paddled out for sunset—the water was glass-smooth, and everything felt still. Small moment, but grounding.
thought_process: The rain set the tone from the start. I'm always most productive when the weather's like this—fewer distractions, easier to focus on the work that matters. The bridge closure was frustrating but predictable; I've learned to expect infrastructure stress on days like this. I stayed at the lab because leaving early seemed pointless. The afternoon meetings pulled me out of flow, but I needed that social reset. The walk to QFC wasn't really about coffee—it was about breaking the fluorescent-lit spell. By evening, I was restless in a good way. The paddle felt necessary, almost therapeutic. Weather shapes everything for me, not just logistically but emotionally. Clear skies, a smooth lake, and honest work—that's the kind of Tuesday that resets the week.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
